_publications._publicationTitle: Modulation of Ras signaling alters the toxicity of hydroquinone, a benzene metabolite and component of cigarette smoke.

_contextSnippet: there was a significantly greater induction of MN in the Nf1−/− cultures than the WT cultures at both 50 μM (p < 0.01) and 100 μM (p < 0.05)
resourceName: Mx1-Cre, Nf1 flox/flox mice (129SV × C57BL/6 J)
details: Nf1−/− cultures showed significantly greater micronucleus formation than WT cultures at 50 μM (p < 0.01) and 100 μM (p < 0.05) hydroquinone treatment
